title: Psalms, Chapter 60
description: Psalms chapter 60. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 103. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.
introduction:
This entry meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library, with the manuscript text drawn directly from the same source as the scan:

  1. Manuscript scan: The actual folio of Codex Sinaiticus containing this passage, from the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). Library folio reference: 103.
  2. Original text: The verbatim transcription of the Sinaiticus manuscript from the Codex Sinaiticus Project, preserving uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra (e.g. ⟨θϲ⟩ = θεός), and scribal marks. Nomina sacra are shown in angle brackets to indicate the manuscript's sacred-name abbreviations.
  3. English translation: The King James Version (1611), retrieved verbatim from the public-domain bible-api.com endpoint.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>ειϲ το τελοϲ εν υμνοιϲ ψαλμοϲ τω δαυειδʼ <sup>2</sup>ειϲακουϲον ο θϲ τηϲ δεηϲεωϲ μου προϲχεϲ τη προϲευχη μου <sup>3</sup>απο των περατων τηϲ γηϲ προϲ ϲε εκεκραξα εν τω ακηδιαϲαι την καρδιαν μου εν πετρα ϋψωϲαϲ με <sup>4</sup>ωδηγηϲαϲ με οτι εγενηθηϲ ελπιϲ μου πυργοϲ ϊϲχυοϲ απο προϲωπου εχθρου <sup>5</sup>παροικηϲω εν τω ϲκηνωματι ϲου ειϲ τουϲ αιωναϲ διαψαλμα ϲκεπαϲθηϲομαι εν ϲκεπη των πτερυγων ϲου
translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>O God, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; O turn thyself to us again. <sup>2</sup>Thou hast made the earth to tremble; thou hast broken it: heal the breaches thereof; for it shaketh. <sup>3</sup>Thou hast shewed thy people hard things: thou hast made us to drink the wine of astonishment. <sup>4</sup>Thou hast given a banner to them that fear thee, that it may be displayed because of the truth. Selah. <sup>5</sup>That thy beloved may be delivered; save
with thy right hand, and hear me.

translation.original_text: <sup>6</sup>οτι ϲυ ο θϲ ειϲηκουϲαϲ τηϲ προϲευχηϲ μου εδωκαϲ κληρονομιαν τοιϲ φοβουμενοιϲ ο ονομα ϲου <sup>7</sup>ημεραϲ εφ ημεραϲ βαϲιλεωϲ προϲθηϲειϲ ετη αυτου εωϲ ημεραϲ γενεαϲ και γενεαϲ <sup>8</sup>διαμενει ειϲ τον αιωνα ενωπιον του θυ ελεοϲ και αληθειαν αυτου τιϲ εκζητηϲει <sup>9</sup>ουτωϲ ψαλω τω ονοματι ϲου ειϲ το αιωνα του αιωνοϲ του αποδουναι με ταϲ ευχαϲ μου ημεραν εξ ημεραϲ
translation.text:
<sup>6</sup>God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth. <sup>7</sup>Gilead
is mine, and Manasseh
is mine; Ephraim also
is the strength of mine head; Judah
is my lawgiver; <sup>8</sup>Moab
is my washpot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe: Philistia, triumph thou because of me. <sup>9</sup>Who will bring me
into the strong city? who will lead me into Edom? <sup>10</sup>Wilt not thou, O God,
which hadst cast us off? and
thou, O God,
which didst not go out with our armies?

<sup>11</sup>Give us help from trouble: for vain
is the help of man. <sup>12</sup>Through God we shall do valiantly: for he
it is that shall tread down our enemies.
